{"additions":4987,"baseRefName":"main","body":"## Context

Integrates a Telegram bot into MyPocket for expense registration, CSV import, and budget queries via chat. Also adds a centralized deduplication module that prevents duplicate transactions across both the bot and web app.

### What's included

- **Deduplication module** (`src/lib/dedup.ts`): Centralized duplicate detection (exact match on description + amount + same day). Integrated into existing CSV import endpoint.
- **Telegram bot core** (`src/lib/telegram/`): API client, update router, message parser, command handlers, and query formatters — all using raw `fetch` (no external Telegram lib).
- **Webhook endpoint** (`/api/telegram/webhook`): Secret-validated POST endpoint with auth middleware exception.
- **Account linking** (`/api/telegram/link`): Deep link flow with 10-minute expiring tokens. `/start` command validates and creates link.
- **Expense registration**: Parse free-text messages (`"PIX RESTAURANTE 45,90 05/03/2026"`), auto-categorize, confirm via inline keyboard with category change support.
- **CSV import via bot**: Send `.csv` file → bot parses, deduplicates, shows summary → confirm to import.
- **Query commands**: `/menu` with summary, category breakdown, and paginated transaction history.
- **Settings UI**: New "Telegram" tab with link/unlink functionality.

### New environment variables required

- `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` — Bot token from @BotFather
- `TELEGRAM_BOT_USERNAME` — Bot username (without @)
- `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — Secret for webhook validation
- `TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET` — Secret for the setup endpoint

### Schema changes (additive only)

3 new Prisma models: `TelegramLink`, `TelegramLinkToken`, `TelegramPendingImport`. No changes to existing models.

### Test coverage

28 new tests: dedup (5), parser (7), link API (8), commands (8). All passing. 3 new integration tests for import dedup.","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDORDToqM7vglNh","author":{"login":"vercel"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"[vc]: #REDACTED:REDACTED

+# Bot Telegram MyPocket — Plano de Implementação
+
+## Overview
+
+Integrar um bot Telegram ao MyPocket para registrar despesas, importar faturas CSV e consultar orçamentos/gastos via chat. Inclui deduplicação centralizada para evitar transações duplicadas tanto no bot quanto no app web.
+
+## Current State Analysis
+
+- **Sem deduplicação:** importar o mesmo CSV duas vezes cria duplicatas (`src/app/api/import/route.ts:166-186` cria transações incondicionalmente)
+- **Sem integração Telegram:** nenhum modelo, endpoint ou lógica de bot existe
+- **Settings extensível:** página de settings (`src/app/settings/page.tsx:528-554`) usa tabs Radix — nova aba é direta
+- **Middleware restritivo:** `src/auth.config.ts:11-28` bloqueia todas as rotas não-auth — webhook precisa de exceção
+- **Summary endpoint reutilizável:** `GET /api/summary` retorna totais, breakdown por categoria e alertas de orçamento — ideal para consultas do bot
+- **Categorizer reutilizável:** `src/lib/categorizer.ts:95-109` faz auto-categorização por keywords — bot pode usar diretamente
+- **CSV parser reutilizável:** `src/lib/csv-parser.ts:151` detecta banco e parseia — bot pode chamar diretamente
+
+## Desired End State
+
+1. Módulo de deduplicação centralizado previne transações duplicatas em toda a aplicação
+2. Bot Telegram funcional com: vinculação por deep link, registro de despesas via texto, importação CSV, consultas de resumo/categorias/transações
+3. Nova aba "Telegram" na página de settings com botão de vincular/desvincular
+4. Webhook seguro integrado ao Next.js sem dependências externas pesadas
+
...
</persisted-output>

@@ -0,0 +1,179 @@
+# Bot Telegram MyPocket — Plano de Implementação
+
+## Overview
+
+Integrar um bot Telegram ao MyPocket para registrar despesas, importar faturas CSV e consultar orçamentos/gastos via chat. Inclui deduplicação centralizada para evitar transações duplicadas tanto no bot quanto no app web.
+
+## Current State Analysis
+
+- **Sem deduplicação:** importar o mesmo CSV duas vezes cria duplicatas (`src/app/api/import/route.ts:166-186` cria transações incondicionalmente)
+- **Sem integração Telegram:** nenhum modelo, endpoint ou lógica de bot existe
+- **Settings extensível:** página de settings (`src/app/settings/page.tsx:528-554`) usa tabs Radix — nova aba é direta
+- **Middleware restritivo:** `src/auth.config.ts:11-28` bloqueia todas as rotas não-auth — webhook precisa de exceção
+- **Summary endpoint reutilizável:** `GET /api/summary` retorna totais, breakdown por categoria e alertas de orçamento — ideal para consultas do bot
+- **Categorizer reutilizável:** `src/lib/categorizer.ts:95-109` faz auto-categorização por keywords — bot pode usar diretamente
+- **CSV parser reutilizável:** `src/lib/csv-parser.ts:151` detecta banco e parseia — bot pode chamar diretamente
+
+## Desired End State
+
+1. Módulo de deduplicação centralizado previne transações duplicatas em toda a aplicação
+2. Bot Telegram funcional com: vinculação por deep link, registro de despesas via texto, importação CSV, consultas de resumo/categorias/transações
+3. Nova aba "Telegram" na página de settings com botão de vincular/desvincular
+4. Webhook seguro integrado ao Next.js sem dependências externas pesadas
+
+**Verificação:** Enviar mensagem de texto com dados de transação no Telegram → transação aparece no dashboard web. Importar mesmo CSV via bot e via web → sem duplicatas.
+
+## What We're NOT Doing
+
+- Interface de IA/NLP — apenas comandos e formatos estruturados
+- Notificações proativas do bot (alertas de orçamento, lembretes)
+- Suporte a WhatsApp ou outros mensageiros
+- OCR de prints/fotos via bot (apenas texto e CSV)
+- Paginação server-side no endpoint de transações existente
+
+---
+
+## Related Documentation
+
+| Document | Relevância |
+|----------|-----------|
+| `ai_docs/brainstorm/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-design.md` | Design validado com todas as decisões |
+| `ai_docs/research/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-research.md` | Pesquisa detalhada do codebase |
+| `ai_docs/diagrams/api-flows.md` | Fluxos de API existentes |
+| `ai_docs/diagrams/data-models.md` | Diagrama ER das entidades |
+| `src/app/api/import/route.ts` | Fluxo de importação a ser modificado com dedup |
+| `src/app/api/summary/route.ts` | Queries do dashboard a reutilizar para consultas do bot |
+
+**Scope Warning:** Este plano cobre o bot Telegram, dedup, e a aba de settings. Não assume comportamento de investments, recurring expenses, ou OCR.
+
+---
+
+## Risks & Mitigations
+
+| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
+|------|------------|--------|------------|
+| Telegram webhook não alcançável em dev local | H | M | Usar ngrok/localtunnel para expor URL local; endpoint de setup aceita URL customizada |
+| Dedup falso positivo (pular transação legítima) | L | H | Match exato (description case-insensitive + amount + date mesmo dia) — conservador por design |
+| Middleware bloqueia webhook requests | M | H | Adicionar exceção explícita em auth.config.ts; testar com request sem session |
+| Telegram File API timeout para CSVs grandes | L | M | Limitar tamanho de arquivo (20MB — limite do Telegram); timeout generoso no fetch |
+| Schema migration quebra banco existente | L | H | Novos modelos apenas (additive) — sem alteração em modelos existentes |
+
+---
+
+## Phases
+
+| # | Phase | File | Summary | Status | Depends On |
+|---|-------|------|---------|--------|------------|
+| 1 | Schema + Deduplicação | `phase-1-schema-dedup.md` | Modelos Prisma + módulo dedup + integração na importação | complete | — |
+| 2 | Core do Bot + Webhook | `phase-2-bot-core.md` | Cliente Telegram, webhook endpoint, handler principal | complete | Phase 1 |
+| 3 | Vinculação via Deep Link | `phase-3-linking.md` | API de tokens, comando /start, aba Settings | complete | Phase 2 |
+| 4 | Registro de Despesas | `phase-4-expense-registration.md` | Parser de mensagens, confirmação com keyboard, criação de transação | complete | Phase 3 |
+| 5 | CSV Import + Consultas | `phase-5-csv-queries.md` | Importação CSV via bot, menu de consultas | complete | Phase 4 |
+
+---
+
+## Success Criteria Dashboard
+
+### Phase 1: Schema + Deduplicação
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(running-automated-checks)` | auto | pass |
+| `npm run db:generate` executa sem erros | auto | pass |
+
+### Phase 2: Core do Bot + Webhook
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(running-automated-checks)` | auto | pass |
+
+### Phase 3: Vinculação via Deep Link
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(running-automated-checks)` | auto | pass |
+| Vincular conta via deep link no Telegram (requer bot token real) | manual | pending |
+
+### Phase 4: Registro de Despesas
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(running-automated-checks)` | auto | pass |
+
+### Phase 5: CSV Import + Consultas
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(running-automated-checks)` | auto | pass |
+| Enviar CSV via Telegram e verificar importação no dashboard | manual | pending |
+
+### Final Verification (after all phases)
+| Criteria | Type | Status |
+|----------|------|--------|
+| `Skill(reviewing-code)` | auto | pass |
+| `Skill(validating-implementation)` | auto | pending |
+
+---
+
+## Known Executor Risks
+
+| Scenario | Likelihood | Fallback |
+|----------|------------|----------|
+| Prisma migration path differs between PostgreSQL and SQLite | M | Usar `db:push` em dev, `db:migrate` para criação formal |
+| `parseCSV` requer `origin` como segundo parâmetro | M | Verificar signature antes de chamar; origin pode ser "Telegram" |
+| Settings page é muito grande (~1200 linhas) | H | Executor deve usar Edit cirúrgico com strings únicas para inserir tab |
+| Telegram API types não disponíveis sem lib | L | Definir tipos inline para Update, Message, CallbackQuery |
+| Setup endpoint chamado por usuário não-admin | M | Endpoint protegido por `TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET` header |
+
+---
+
+## Completeness Verification
+
+```
+100% Rule Verification:
+- [x] Every file mentioned in research is addressed in plan
+- [x] No work will be discovered during implementation
+- [x] All dependencies between tasks are explicit
+- [x] Each phase is independently shippable
+- [x] All tasks have Given-When-Then acceptance criteria
+- [x] All tasks have Reference Docs listed
+- [x] No gaps between phases (nothing falls through cracks)
+- [x] No overlaps (no work duplicated across tasks)
+```
+
+---
+
+## Implementation Notes
+
+### Padrões a seguir
+- API routes: `getAuthenticatedUserId()` para rotas autenticadas, validação de `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` para webhook
+- Prisma queries sempre filtram por `userId`
+- Textos de UI e respostas do bot em PT-BR
+- Amounts: negativo para despesas, positivo para receitas
+- Datas: append `T12:00:00` para evitar timezone issues
+
+### Telegram API
+- Usar `fetch` direto ao invés de dependência externa
+- Base URL: `https://api.telegram.org/bot${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}/`
+- Webhook secret via header `X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token`
+- File download: `https://api.telegram.org/file/bot${TOKEN}/${file_path}`
+
+### Code Design Principles
+**Project rules are the source of truth.** Before writing code, load:
+1. `CLAUDE.md` in project root
+2. `$HOME/.claude/docs/code-principles.md` (fallback)
+
+---
+
+## Learnings
+
+1. **Prisma/DB:** Projeto usa PostgreSQL com Neon adapter (driverAdapters preview). `db:generate` funciona sem DATABASE_URL mas `db:push` e `build` requerem a variável.
+2. **Dedup:** Match exato (description case-insensitive + amount + mesmo dia) é conservador e evita falsos positivos.
+3. **Middleware:** Exceção no auth usa `===` (match exato) ao invés de `startsWith` para evitar bypass em sub-rotas.
+4. **Telegram sem lib:** Tipos definidos inline, `fetch` direto para API — funcional sem dependência externa.
+5. **Settings page:** ~1200 linhas — edição cirúrgica com strings de contexto únicas funciona bem.
+6. **Category change flow:** `handleShowCategories` e `handleSetCategory` precisam preservar as linhas de dados (📝💰📅) da despesa no texto da mensagem, senão `handleConfirmExpense` não consegue parsear os dados após troca de categoria.
+7. **MapIterator spread:** `[...map.entries()]` não funciona com o target ES do projeto; usar `Array.from(map.entries())`.
+
+---
+
+## References
+
+- Research: `../research/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-research.md`
+- Brainstorm: `../brainstorm/2026-03-07-telegram-bot-design.md`
+- Similar: `src/app/api/import/route.ts` (import flow), `src/app/api/summary/route.ts` (dashboard queries)
diff --git a/ai_docs/plans/2026-03-07-telegram-bot/phase-1-schema-dedup.md b/ai_docs/plans/2026-03-07-telegram-bot/phase-1-schema-dedup.md

@@ -0,0 +1,469 @@
+# Phase 1: Schema + Deduplicação
+
+## Overview
+
+Adicionar modelos Prisma para vinculação Telegram, criar módulo centralizado de deduplicação de transações, e integrá-lo ao fluxo de importação existente. Esta fase entrega valor imediato — deduplicação funciona no app web independente do bot.
+
+## Reference Docs for This Phase
+- `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 16-49) - User model com relations
+- `prisma/schema.prisma` (lines 414-435) - Final do schema (onde adicionar novos modelos)
+- `src/app/api/import/route.ts` (lines 80-187) - Loop de criação de transações

+```gherkin

+Then it returns null (ignores deleted transactions)

+**Implementation**:

+`src/lib/dedup.ts`:
+```typescript
+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
+
+export interface DuplicateCheckParams {
+  userId: string
+  description: string
+  amount: number
+  date: Date
+}
+
+export async function findDuplicate({
+  userId,
+  description,
+  amount,
+  date,
+}: DuplicateCheckParams) {
+  // Normalize date to start/end of day
+  const dayStart = new Date(date)
+  dayStart.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)
+  const dayEnd = new Date(date)
+  dayEnd.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999)
+
+  const duplicate = await prisma.transaction.findFirst({
+    where: {
+      userId,
+      description: {
+        equals: description.trim(),
+        mode: "insensitive",
+      },
+      amount,
+      date: {
+        gte: dayStart,
+        lte: dayEnd,
+      },
+      deletedAt: null,
+    },
+  })
+
+  return duplicate
+}
+
+export async function filterDuplicates(
+  userId: string,
+  transactions: Array<{ description: string; amount: number; date: Date }>
+): Promise<{ unique: typeof transactions; duplicateCount: number }> {
+  const unique: typeof transactions = []
+  let duplicateCount = 0
+
+  for (const t of transactions) {
+    const dup = await findDuplicate({
+      userId,
+      description: t.description,
+      amount: t.amount,
+      date: t.date,
+    })
+    if (dup) {
+      duplicateCount++
+    } else {
+      unique.push(t)
+    }
+  }
+
+  return { unique, duplicateCount }
+}
+```
+

content.text:
The files are in the PR diff but not on the current branch yet. I have enough information from the full diff to provide a thorough review. Let me compile my findings.

---

## Code Review: Telegram Bot Integration PR

### CRITICAL

**1. Setup endpoint missing middleware exception -- accessible but unprotected against auth redirect**

The `/api/telegram/setup` endpoint is protected by `x-setup-secret` header, but it's NOT added to the middleware exception list in `auth.config.ts`. Only `/api/telegram/webhook` was added. This means unauthenticated requests to `/api/telegram/setup` will be redirected to login by the middleware before the route handler ever runs. The setup endpoint will be unusable when called via `curl` without a session cookie.

**File:** `/src/auth.config.ts`

**Fix:** Either add `/api/telegram/setup` to the middleware exceptions (with the `===` exact match pattern already used), or document that setup must be done by an authenticated user (and use `getAuthenticatedUserId` instead of the custom secret header).

---

**2. `handleCsvConfirm` does NOT verify that the pending import belongs to the requesting user**

In `commands.ts`, `handleCsvConfirm` fetches the pending import by `id` only and never checks `pending.userId === userId`. A malicious user who knows/guesses the `importId` (a cuid) could confirm another user's pending import, causing transactions to be created under `userId` (from the bot link) rather than the original uploader. While the `userId` used for `prisma.transaction.create` comes from the bot link (not the pending import), the actual transaction data (payload) comes from the pending import of a potentially different user.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleCsvConfirm` function

**Fix:** Add `if (pending.userId !== userId)` check after fetching the pending import.

---

**3. Webhook secret comparison is not timing-safe**

In `/src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, the webhook secret is compared using `!==` (strict equality). This is vulnerable to timing attacks that could allow an attacker to brute-force the secret byte by byte.

**File:** `/src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

**Fix:** Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for the comparison:
```typescript
import { timingSafeEqual } from "crypto"
const expected = Buffer.from(process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET || "")
const received = Buffer.from(secret || "")
if (expected.length !== received.length || !timingSafeEqual(expected, received)) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 })
}
```

---

### IMPORTANT

**4. `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` evaluated at module load time in `client.ts`**

Line 1 of `client.ts`:
```typescript
const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`
```

This is evaluated when the module is first imported, which happens at build/startup time. If the env var isn't set at that moment, the URL will contain `undefined`. This is a common Next.js footgun -- if the module gets imported during build (SSG/ISR), the token will be baked in as `undefined`.

**Fix:** Use a getter function or lazy initialization:
```typescript
function getTelegramApi() {
  return `https://api.telegram.org/bot${process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`
}
```

---

**5. `handleConfirmExpense` parses transaction data from message text -- fragile and manipulable**

The `handleConfirmExpense` function extracts description, amount, and date by parsing emoji-prefixed lines from the Telegram message text. This has two problems:

a) **Fragility:** If `formatCurrency` output changes format (e.g., uses non-breaking spaces or different separators), the regex `.replace("R$", "").replace(/\./g, "").replace(",", ".")` will break silently and produce `NaN`.

b) **Manipulation risk:** Telegram's `editMessageText` API doesn't prevent a sophisticated attacker from crafting a callback query with a modified `message.text`. The Telegram Bot API includes the message in callback queries, but it's the *current* message text, which the bot itself wrote. However, if Telegram's callback payload is ever spoofed (the webhook secret mitigates this), the amount could be manipulated.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleConfirmExpense`

**Suggestion:** Store the parsed expense data in the database (like `TelegramPendingImport` is used for CSV) or in the callback_data itself (if it fits within 64 bytes). Parsing from message text is a code smell.

---

**6. `handleSetCategory` does not validate that the category belongs to the user**

In `handleSetCategory`, `prisma.category.findUnique({ where: { id: categoryId } })` fetches any category by ID without filtering by `userId`. A user could potentially set a category belonging to another user by crafting a callback with a known category ID.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleSetCategory`

**Fix:** Add `userId` to the query, but this requires passing `userId` through. Currently the function doesn't receive `userId`.

---

**7. `handleShowCategories` does not filter investment-related categories**

The category query fetches all categories for the user. If there are categories used exclusively for investments or transfers, they'll show up in the expense category picker. This is a minor UX issue but worth noting.

---

**8. CSV import via bot skips recurring expense matching, carryover detection, and `importBatchId`**

The web import at `/src/app/api/import/route.ts` does: recurring expense matching, carryover detection, category tag validation, and assigns an `importBatchId`. The bot's `handleCsvConfirm` skips ALL of these. This means:
- CSV imports via bot cannot be batch-undone (no `importBatchId`)
- Recurring expenses won't be linked
- Carryover transactions won't be detected

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleCsvConfirm`

**Suggestion:** Extract the import loop from `/src/app/api/import/route.ts` into a shared service function that both the API route and the bot can call. This would prevent feature drift.

---

**9. `filterDuplicates` function is defined but never used**

The exported `filterDuplicates` in `dedup.ts` is not used anywhere in the codebase. Both the import route and the bot manually loop and call `findDuplicate` individually.

**File:** `/src/lib/dedup.ts`

---

**10. N+1 query problem in dedup checking**

Both the import route and the bot's CSV handler call `findDuplicate` inside a loop, issuing one `findFirst` query per transaction. For a CSV with 200 transactions, this means 200 individual database queries. 

**Suggestion:** Batch the dedup check by fetching all potential duplicates for the date range in a single query, then matching in memory.

---

### MINOR

**11. `handleUnlinkCommand` allows unlinking via bot without confirmation**

`/desvincular` immediately deletes the link. No confirmation step. A user who accidentally types the command loses their link. Consider adding an inline keyboard confirmation like the expense flow does.

---

**12. `parseCSV` called with hardcoded origin "Telegram"**

In `handleDocumentMessage`, the origin is set to `"Telegram"`. However, the CSV may come from C6, Itau, or BTG. The `parseCSV` function detects the bank format, but the origin passed to the function and later stored is always "Telegram". The web import uses the actual detected origin. This means recurring expense matching (if added later) won't work because origin won't match.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleDocumentMessage`

---

**13. `handleSummaryQuery` casts transaction to `Record<string, unknown>` for investment check**

```typescript
if ((t as Record<string, unknown>).investmentTransaction) continue
```

This is a type assertion workaround. The `include: { investmentTransaction: true }` should give proper typing. The cast suggests a type mismatch that should be fixed properly.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/queries.ts`

---

**14. Dead code: `callbackData` variable in `handleExpenseMessage`**

In the plan's Phase 4 implementation, a `callbackData` JSON.stringify is built but never used (the code uses `confirmData` with just the categoryId instead). The actual implementation in `commands.ts` seems to have removed this, but the plan doc still shows it.

---

**15. Settings page: Telegram link status does not auto-refresh after linking**

After clicking the deep link and completing linking in Telegram, the Settings page still shows "Conta nao vinculada" until manually refreshed. There's no polling or WebSocket to detect when linking completes.

---

### NIT

**16. Test file placement inconsistency**

`link-api.test.ts` is placed in `src/lib/telegram/` but tests an API route at `src/app/api/telegram/link/route.ts`. Per project convention, API integration tests go in `tests/integration/`. The parser and command tests being co-located in `src/lib/telegram/` is fine since they test library code.

---

**17. `downloadFile` doesn't check response status**

In `client.ts`, `downloadFile` calls `res.text()` without checking `res.ok`. If the file download fails (e.g., file expired, Telegram 400), it will return an HTML error page as the CSV content, which `parseCSV` will then fail to parse.

---

**18. `pending!.origin` -- unnecessary non-null assertion**

In `handleCsvConfirm`, `pending!.origin` uses a non-null assertion, but `pending` is already verified as non-null by the guard at the top of the function.

---

### Summary

The overall architecture is solid -- good separation between client, bot router, commands, and queries. The webhook security model (secret token + middleware exception) is correct in principle. The main concerns are:

1. **Security:** Missing userId validation in `handleCsvConfirm` and `handleSetCategory`, timing-unsafe secret comparison
2. **Feature parity:** Bot CSV import lacks `importBatchId`, recurring matching, and carryover detection -- consider extracting shared import logic
3. **Robustness:** Module-level env var evaluation, fragile message text parsing for expense confirmation, no response status checking on Telegram API calls
4. **Performance:** N+1 dedup queries for large imports

The files are in the PR diff but not on the current branch yet. I have enough information from the full diff to provide a thorough review. Let me compile my findings.

---

## Code Review: Telegram Bot Integration PR

### CRITICAL

**1. Setup endpoint missing middleware exception -- accessible but unprotected against auth redirect**

The `/api/telegram/setup` endpoint is protected by `x-setup-secret` header, but it's NOT added to the middleware exception list in `auth.config.ts`. Only `/api/telegram/webhook` was added. This means unauthenticated requests to `/api/telegram/setup` will be redirected to login by the middleware before the route handler ever runs. The setup endpoint will be unusable when called via `curl` without a session cookie.

**File:** `/src/auth.config.ts`

**Fix:** Either add `/api/telegram/setup` to the middleware exceptions (with the `===` exact match pattern already used), or document that setup must be done by an authenticated user (and use `getAuthenticatedUserId` instead of the custom secret header).

---

**2. `handleCsvConfirm` does NOT verify that the pending import belongs to the requesting user**

In `commands.ts`, `handleCsvConfirm` fetches the pending import by `id` only and never checks `pending.userId === userId`. A malicious user who knows/guesses the `importId` (a cuid) could confirm another user's pending import, causing transactions to be created under `userId` (from the bot link) rather than the original uploader. While the `userId` used for `prisma.transaction.create` comes from the bot link (not the pending import), the actual transaction data (payload) comes from the pending import of a potentially different user.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleCsvConfirm` function

**Fix:** Add `if (pending.userId !== userId)` check after fetching the pending import.

---

**3. Webhook secret comparison is not timing-safe**

In `/src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`, the webhook secret is compared using `!==` (strict equality). This is vulnerable to timing attacks that could allow an attacker to brute-force the secret byte by byte.

**File:** `/src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

**Fix:** Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for the comparison:
```typescript
import { timingSafeEqual } from "crypto"
const expected = Buffer.from(process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET || "")
const received = Buffer.from(secret || "")
if (expected.length !== received.length || !timingSafeEqual(expected, received)) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 })
}
```

---

### IMPORTANT

```typescript
  return `https://api.telegram.org/bot${process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}`
}
```

b) **Manipulation risk:** Telegram's `editMessageText` API doesn't prevent a sophisticated attacker from crafting a callback query with a modified `message.text`. The Telegram Bot API includes the message in callback queries, but it's the *current* message text, which the bot itself wrote. However, if Telegram's callback payload is ever spoofed (the webhook secret mitigates this), the amount could be manipulated.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleConfirmExpense`

**Suggestion:** Store the parsed expense data in the database (like `TelegramPendingImport` is used for CSV) or in the callback_data itself (if it fits within 64 bytes). Parsing from message text is a code smell.

---

**6. `handleSetCategory` does not validate that the category belongs to the user**

In `handleSetCategory`, `prisma.category.findUnique({ where: { id: categoryId } })` fetches any category by ID without filtering by `userId`. A user could potentially set a category belonging to another user by crafting a callback with a known category ID.

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleSetCategory`

**Fix:** Add `userId` to the query, but this requires passing `userId` through. Currently the function doesn't receive `userId`.

**7. `handleShowCategories` does not filter investment-related categories**

---

**8. CSV import via bot skips recurring expense matching, carryover detection, and `importBatchId`**

The web import at `/src/app/api/import/route.ts` does: recurring expense matching, carryover detection, category tag validation, and assigns an `importBatchId`. The bot's `handleCsvConfirm` skips ALL of these. This means:
- CSV imports via bot cannot be batch-undone (no `importBatchId`)
- Recurring expenses won't be linked
- Carryover transactions won't be detected

**File:** `/src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` -- `handleCsvConfirm`

**Suggestion:** Extract the import loop from `/src/app/api/import/route.ts` into a shared service function that both the API route and the bot can call. This would prevent feature drift.

---

**9. `filterDuplicates` function is defined but never used**

The exported `filterDuplicates` in `dedup.ts` is not used anywhere in the codebase. Both the import route and the bot manually loop and call `findDuplicate` individually.

---

**15. Settings page: Telegram link status does not auto-refresh after linking**

After clicking the deep link and completing linking in Telegram, the Settings page still shows "Conta nao vinculada" until manually refreshed. There's no polling or WebSocket to detect when linking completes.

---

### NIT

**16. Test file placement inconsistency**

`link-api.test.ts` is placed in `src/lib/telegram/` but tests an API route at `src/app/api/telegram/link/route.ts`. Per project convention, API integration tests go in `tests/integration/`. The parser and command tests being co-located in `src/lib/telegram/` is fine since they test library code.

---

**17. `downloadFile` doesn't check response status**

In `client.ts`, `downloadFile` calls `res.text()` without checking `res.ok`. If the file download fails (e.g., file expired, Telegram 400), it will return an HTML error page as the CSV content, which `parseCSV` will then fail to parse.

---

In `handleCsvConfirm`, `pending!.origin` uses a non-null assertion, but `pending` is already verified as non-null by the guard at the top of the function.

---

### Summary

The overall architecture is solid -- good separation between client, bot router, commands, and queries. The webhook security model (secret token + middleware exception) is correct in principle. The main concerns are:

1. **Security:** Missing userId validation in `handleCsvConfirm` and `handleSetCategory`, timing-unsafe secret comparison
2. **Feature parity:** Bot CSV import lacks `importBatchId`, recurring matching, and carryover detection -- consider extracting shared import logic
3. **Robustness:** Module-level env var evaluation, fragile message text parsing for expense confirmation, no response status checking on Telegram API calls
4. **Performance:** N+1 dedup queries for large imports

## Review do PR #12 — Telegram Bot Integration

### CRITICAL

**1. `handleCsvConfirm` não valida que o import pertence ao usuário solicitante**
Em `commands.ts`, o pending import é buscado apenas por `id`, sem checar `pending.userId === userId`. Um usuário que adivinhe o `importId` (cuid) pode confirmar o import de outro usuário.

**2. Comparação do webhook secret não é timing-safe**
Em `webhook/route.ts`, o secret é comparado com `!==`. Vulnerável a timing attacks. Deve usar `crypto.timingSafeEqual`.

**3. Endpoint `/api/telegram/setup` sem exceção no middleware**
Só `/api/telegram/webhook` foi adicionado às exceções em `auth.config.ts`. O setup endpoint será redirecionado para login quando chamado via curl sem session cookie.

---

### IMPORTANT

**4. `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` avaliado em tempo de carregamento do módulo**
`client.ts` linha 1 constrói a URL com `process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` no top-level. Se o módulo for importado durante build, o token será `undefined`. Usar getter/lazy init.

**5. `handleConfirmExpense` parseia dados da transação do texto da mensagem**
Frágil — depende do formato exato de `formatCurrency`. Se mudar separador ou espaço, o regex quebra silenciosamente e produz `NaN`. Melhor armazenar os dados no banco (como `TelegramPendingImport`) ou no `callback_data`.

**6. `handleSetCategory` não valida que a categoria pertence ao usuário**
`prisma.category.findUnique({ where: { id: categoryId } })` busca qualquer categoria por ID sem filtrar por `userId`.

**7. Import via bot sem paridade com import web**
O import via bot pula: `importBatchId`, matching de despesas recorrentes, detecção de carryover. Imports pelo bot não podem ser desfeitos em batch. Recomendo extrair a lógica de import para uma função compartilhada.

**8. N+1 queries na deduplicação**
`findDuplicate` é chamado individualmente por transação dentro de um loop. Para um CSV com 200 linhas = 200 queries. Fazer batch fetch por range de datas.

---

### MINOR

**9. `downloadFile` não checa `res.ok`** — se o download falhar, retorna HTML de erro como conteúdo CSV.

**10. Origin hardcoded como "Telegram"** — o CSV pode ser de C6/Itaú/BTG, mas o origin é sempre "Telegram". Incompatível com matching de recorrentes.

**11. `/desvincular` sem confirmação** — deleta o link imediatamente, sem pedir confirmação ao usuário.

**12. `filterDuplicates` exportada mas nunca usada** em `dedup.ts`.

**13. `link-api.test.ts` deveria estar em `tests/integration/`** — testa uma API route, não código de lib.

---

### Veredicto

A arquitetura geral é boa — separação clara entre client, bot router, commands e queries. Os pontos críticos são:
- **Segurança:** falta validação de `userId` em `handleCsvConfirm` e `handleSetCategory`, comparação de secret não timing-safe
- **Paridade de features:** import via bot diverge significativamente do web
- **Robustez:** env var avaliada em build time, parsing frágil do texto da mensagem

Recomendo corrigir os 3 críticos antes do merge.

- Telegram bot core (API client, update router, message parser, command handlers)
- Webhook endpoint
- Account linking flow
- Expense registration via text
- CSV import via bot
- Query commands
- Settings UI for link/unlink

Review for:
1. **Security issues** - especially around the webhook, auth, token handling, account linking
2. **Code quality** - patterns, error handling, edge cases
3. **Architecture** - separation of concerns, reusability
4. **Bugs or logic errors**
5. **Test coverage gaps**
6. **Performance concerns**

     7→+# Bot Telegram MyPocket — Plano de Implementação
     8→+
     9→+## Overview
    10→+
    11→+Integrar um bot Telegram ao MyPocket para registrar despesas, importar faturas CSV e consultar orçamentos/gastos via chat. Inclui deduplicação centralizada para evitar transações duplicadas tanto no bot quanto no app web.
    12→+
    13→+## Current State Analysis
    14→+
    15→+- **Sem deduplicação:** importar o mesmo CSV duas vezes cria duplicatas (`src/app/api/import/route.ts:166-186` cria transações incondicionalmente)
    16→+- **Sem integração Telegram:** nenhum modelo, endpoint ou lógica de bot existe
    17→+- **Settings extensível:** página de settings (`src/app/settings/page.tsx:528-554`) usa tabs Radix — nova aba é direta
    18→+- **Middleware restritivo:** `src/auth.config.ts:11-28` bloqueia todas as rotas não-auth — webhook precisa de exceção
    19→+- **Summary endpoint reutilizável:** `GET /api/summary` retorna totais, breakdown por categoria e alertas de orçamento — ideal para consultas do bot
    20→+- **Categorizer reutilizável:** `src/lib/categorizer.ts:95-109` faz auto-categorização por keywords — bot pode usar diretamente
    21→+- **CSV parser reutilizável:** `src/lib/csv-parser.ts:151` detecta banco e parseia — bot pode chamar diretamente
    22→+
    23→+## Desired End State
    24→+
    25→+1. Módulo de deduplicação centralizado previne transações duplicatas em toda a aplicação
    26→+2. Bot Telegram funcional com: vinculação por deep link, registro de despesas via texto, importação CSV, consultas de resumo/categorias/transações
    27→+3. Nova aba "Telegram" na página de settings com botão de vincular/desvincular
    28→+4. Webhook seguro integrado ao Next.js sem dependências externas pesadas
    29→+
    30→+**Verificação:** Enviar mensagem de texto com dados de transação no Telegram → transação aparece no dashboard web. Importar mesmo CSV via bot e via web → sem duplicatas.
    31→+

    36→+- Suporte a WhatsApp ou outros mensageiros
    37→+- OCR de prints/fotos via bot (apenas texto e CSV)

   149→+### Padrões a seguir
   150→+- API routes: `getAuthenticatedUserId()` para rotas autenticadas, validação de `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` para webhook
   151→+- Prisma queries sempre filtram por `userId`

   157→+- Usar `fetch` direto ao invés de dependência externa

   162→+### Code Design Principles
   163→+**Project rules are the source of truth.** Before writing code, load:

   168→+
   169→+## Learnings
   170→+
   171→+1. **Prisma/DB:** Projeto usa PostgreSQL com Neon adapter (driverAdapters preview). `db:generate` funciona sem DATABASE_URL mas `db:push` e `build` requerem a variável.
   172→+2. **Dedup:** Match exato (description case-insensitive + amount + mesmo dia) é conservador e evita falsos positivos.
   173→+3. **Middleware:** Exceção no auth usa `===` (match exato) ao invés de `startsWith` para evitar bypass em sub-rotas.
   174→+4. **Telegram sem lib:** Tipos definidos inline, `fetch` direto para API — funcional sem dependência externa.
   175→+5. **Settings page:** ~1200 linhas — edição cirúrgica com strings de contexto únicas funciona bem.
   176→+6. **Category change flow:** `handleShowCategories` e `handleSetCategory` precisam preservar as linhas de dados (📝💰📅) da despesa no texto da mensagem, senão `handleConfirmExpense` não consegue parsear os dados após troca de categoria.
   177→+7. **MapIterator spread:** `[...map.entries()]` não funciona com o target ES do projeto; usar `Array.from(map.entries())`.

   318→+
   319→+**Why**: Lógica centralizada para detectar transações duplicatas. Usada pelo import web, pelo bot (registro avulso), e pela importação CSV via bot.
   320→+
   321→+**Acceptance Criteria**:
   322→+```gherkin
   323→+Given a transaction exists with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05
   324→+When findDuplicate is called with same description (case-insensitive), same amount, same date
   325→+Then it returns the existing transaction
   326→+
   327→+Given a transaction exists with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05
   328→+When findDuplicate is called with different description "UBER TRIP"
   329→+Then it returns null
   330→+
   331→+Given a transaction exists but is soft-deleted (deletedAt is set)
   332→+When findDuplicate is called with matching description, amount, date
   333→+Then it returns null (ignores deleted transactions)
   334→+
   335→+Given a transaction with description "PIX REST FULANO", amount -45.90, date 2026-03-05T15:30:00
   336→+When findDuplicate is called with same description, same amount, date 2026-03-05T08:00:00
   337→+Then it returns the existing transaction (same day, different time)
   338→+```
   339→+
   340→+**Implementation**:
   341→+
   342→+`src/lib/dedup.ts`:
   343→+```typescript
   344→+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
   345→+
   346→+export interface DuplicateCheckParams {
   347→+  userId: string
   348→+  description: string
   349→+  amount: number
   350→+  date: Date
   351→+}
   352→+
   353→+export async function findDuplicate({
   354→+  userId,
   355→+  description,
   356→+  amount,
   357→+  date,
   358→+}: DuplicateCheckParams) {
   359→+  // Normalize date to start/end of day
   360→+  const dayStart = new Date(date)
   361→+  dayStart.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0)
   362→+  const dayEnd = new Date(date)
   363→+  dayEnd.setHours(23, 59, 59, 999)
   364→+
   365→+  const duplicate = await prisma.transaction.findFirst({
   366→+    where: {
   367→+      userId,
   368→+      description: {
   369→+        equals: description.trim(),
   370→+        mode: "insensitive",
   371→+      },
   372→+      amount,
   373→+      date: {
   374→+        gte: dayStart,
   375→+        lte: dayEnd,
   376→+      },
   377→+      deletedAt: null,
   378→+    },
   379→+  })
   380→+
   381→+  return duplicate
   382→+}
   383→+
   384→+export async function filterDuplicates(
   385→+  userId: string,
   386→+  transactions: Array<{ description: string; amount: number; date: Date }>
   387→+): Promise<{ unique: typeof transactions; duplicateCount: number }> {
   388→+  const unique: typeof transactions = []
   389→+  let duplicateCount = 0
   390→+
   391→+  for (const t of transactions) {
   392→+    const dup = await findDuplicate({
   393→+      userId,
   394→+      description: t.description,
   395→+      amount: t.amount,
   396→+      date: t.date,
   397→+    })
   398→+    if (dup) {
   399→+      duplicateCount++
   400→+    } else {
   401→+      unique.push(t)
   402→+    }
   403→+  }
   404→+
   405→+  return { unique, duplicateCount }
   406→+}
   407→+```

   668→+
   669→+## Overview
   670→+
   671→+Criar o cliente Telegram (wrapper de fetch para a API), o webhook endpoint integrado ao Next.js, e o handler principal que roteia comandos e valida vinculação de conta. Após esta fase, o webhook recebe mensagens do Telegram e responde com mensagem de "conta não vinculada".

   677→+- Telegram Bot API: `https://core.telegram.org/bots/api`

   847→+```typescript
   848→+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
   849→+import { sendMessage, type TelegramUpdate } from "./client"
   850→+
   851→+export async function handleUpdate(update: TelegramUpdate) {
   852→+  const chatId =
   853→+    update.message?.chat.id ??
   854→+    update.callback_query?.message?.chat.id
   855→+
   856→+  if (!chatId) return
   857→+
   858→+  // Look up linked user
   859→+  const link = await prisma.telegramLink.findUnique({
   860→+    where: { chatId: String(chatId) },
   861→+  })
   862→+
   863→+  // Handle /start command (linking) — always allowed, even without link
   864→+  if (update.message?.text?.startsWith("/start")) {
   865→+    const { handleStartCommand } = await import("./commands")
   866→+    return handleStartCommand(update.message, link?.userId ?? null)
   867→+  }
   868→+
   869→+  // All other commands require a linked account
   870→+  if (!link) {
   871→+    return sendMessage(
   872→+      chatId,
   873→+      "Conta não vinculada. Acesse as configurações do MyPocket para vincular sua conta."
   874→+    )
   875→+  }
   876→+
   877→+  const userId = link.userId
   878→+
   879→+  // Route callback queries (inline keyboard buttons)
   880→+  if (update.callback_query) {
   881→+    const { handleCallbackQuery } = await import("./commands")
   882→+    return handleCallbackQuery(update.callback_query, userId)
   883→+  }
   884→+
   885→+  // Route text messages
   886→+  if (update.message?.text) {
   887→+    const text = update.message.text
   888→+
   889→+    if (text === "/menu") {
   890→+      const { handleMenuCommand } = await import("./commands")
   891→+      return handleMenuCommand(chatId, userId)
   892→+    }
   893→+
   894→+    if (text === "/desvincular") {
   895→+      const { handleUnlinkCommand } = await import("./commands")
   896→+      return handleUnlinkCommand(chatId, userId)
   897→+    }
   898→+
   899→+    // Default: try to parse as expense
   900→+    const { handleExpenseMessage } = await import("./commands")
   901→+    return handleExpenseMessage(update.message, userId)
   902→+  }
   903→+
   904→+  // Route document messages (CSV files)
   905→+  if (update.message?.document) {
   906→+    const { handleDocumentMessage } = await import("./commands")
   907→+    return handleDocumentMessage(update.message, userId)
   908→+  }
   909→+}
   910→+```

   920→+#### 2. Create webhook endpoint and add middleware exception
   921→+
   922→+- [x] **COMPLETE**
   923→+  - **Learning:** A exceção no middleware de autenticação usa comparação exata de pathname (`=== "/api/telegram/webhook"`), não `startsWith`, para evitar bypass acidental em outras rotas sob `/api/telegram/`.
   924→+
   925→+**File**: `src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts` (CREATE) + `src/auth.config.ts` (MODIFY)
   926→+**Complexity**: Medium
   927→+**TDD**: NO — HTTP handler wiring
   928→+**Depends On**: Task 1 (bot handler must exist)
   929→+
   930→+**Load Before Implementing**:
   931→+1. `src/auth.config.ts` (full file) - Middleware config to modify
   932→+2. `src/app/api/import/route.ts` (lines 1-5) - API route import pattern
   933→+3. `src/lib/telegram/bot.ts` (full file) - handleUpdate function to call
   934→+
   935→+**Pre-conditions**:
   936→+- [ ] Task 1 completed (`src/lib/telegram/bot.ts` exists)
   937→+- [ ] `src/auth.config.ts` exists with `authorized` callback
   938→+
   939→+**Why**: O webhook é o ponto de entrada para todas as mensagens do Telegram. A exceção no middleware garante que requests do Telegram não são bloqueados pela autenticação JWT.
   940→+
   941→+**Acceptance Criteria**:
   942→+```gherkin
   943→+Given a POST request to /api/telegram/webhook with valid X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header
   944→+When the request contains a valid Telegram update
   945→+Then handleUpdate is called and 200 OK is returned
   946→+
   947→+Given a POST request to /api/telegram/webhook without the secret token header
   948→+When the request is received
   949→+Then 401 Unauthorized is returned
   950→+
   951→+Given the middleware is configured
   952→+When a request comes to /api/telegram/webhook without a session
   953→+Then the request is allowed through (not redirected to login)
   954→+```
   955→+
   956→+**Implementation**:
   957→+
   958→+`src/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`:
   959→+```typescript
   960→+import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
   961→+import { handleUpdate } from "@/lib/telegram/bot"
   962→+import type { TelegramUpdate } from "@/lib/telegram/client"
   963→+
   964→+export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
   965→+  try {
   966→+    // Validate webhook secret
   967→+    const secret = request.headers.get("x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token")
   968→+    if (secret !== process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET) {
   969→+      return NextResponse.json({ error: "Unauthorized" }, { status: 401 })
   970→+    }
   971→+
   972→+    const update: TelegramUpdate = await request.json()
   973→+
   974→+    await handleUpdate(update)
   975→+
   976→+    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true })
   977→+  } catch (error) {
   978→+    console.error("Telegram webhook error:", error)
   979→+    return NextResponse.json(
   980→+      { error: "Internal error" },
   981→+      { status: 500 }
   982→+    )
   983→+  }
   984→+}
   985→+```
   986→+

  1043→+export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
  1044→+  try {
  1045→+    // Admin-only: validate setup secret

  1049→+    }
  1050→+
  1051→+    const { webhookUrl } = await request.json()
  1052→+    if (!webhookUrl) {
  1053→+      return NextResponse.json(

  1055→+        { status: 400 }
  1056→+      )
  1057→+    }
  1058→+
  1059→+    const secret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET
  1060→+    if (!secret) {
  1061→+      return NextResponse.json(
  1062→+        { error: "TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET não configurado" },
  1063→+        { status: 500 }
  1064→+      )
  1065→+    }
  1066→+
  1067→+    const result = await setWebhook(webhookUrl, secret)
  1068→+    return NextResponse.json(result)
  1069→+  } catch (error) {
  1070→+    console.error("Error setting up Telegram webhook:", error)
  1071→+    return NextResponse.json(
  1072→+      { error: "Erro ao configurar webhook" },
  1073→+      { status: 500 }
  1074→+    )
  1075→+  }
  1076→+}

  1162→+# Phase 3: Vinculação via Deep Link
  1163→+
  1164→+## Overview
  1165→+
  1166→+Implementar o fluxo completo de vinculação de conta: API de geração de token, comando `/start TOKEN` no bot, e nova aba "Telegram" na página de Settings com botão de vincular/desvincular. Após esta fase, um usuário pode vincular sua conta MyPocket ao Telegram.
  1167→+
  1168→+## Reference Docs for This Phase
  1169→+- `prisma/schema.prisma` - TelegramLink e TelegramLinkToken models (criados na Phase 1)
  1170→+- `src/lib/telegram/bot.ts` - Handler principal com roteamento (criado na Phase 2)
  1171→+- `src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` - Stubs a substituir (criado na Phase 2)
  1172→+- `src/app/settings/page.tsx` (lines 528-554) - TabsList para adicionar nova aba
  1173→+- `src/app/api/settings/route.ts` - Padrão de API com auth
  1174→+- `src/lib/auth-utils.ts` - getAuthenticatedUserId pattern
  1175→+

  1177→+
  1178→+#### 1. Create link token API endpoint - [x] DONE
  1179→+
  1180→+**File**: `src/app/api/telegram/link/route.ts` (CREATE)
  1181→+**Complexity**: High
  1182→+**TDD**: YES
  1183→+**Depends On**: Phase 1 Task 1 (TelegramLinkToken model must exist)
  1184→+
  1185→+**Load Before Implementing**:
  1186→+1. `prisma/schema.prisma` (TelegramLink and TelegramLinkToken models)
  1187→+2. `src/lib/auth-utils.ts` (full file) - Auth pattern
  1188→+3. `src/app/api/settings/route.ts` (full file) - API route pattern to follow
  1189→+
  1190→+**Pre-conditions**:
  1191→+- [ ] TelegramLinkToken model exists in Prisma schema
  1192→+- [ ] TelegramLink model exists in Prisma schema
  1193→+- [ ] `npm run db:generate` has been run after Phase 1
  1194→+
  1195→+**Why**: Gera tokens temporários para vinculação via deep link. Também provê GET para status de vinculação e DELETE para desvinculação.
  1196→+
  1197→+**Acceptance Criteria**:
  1198→+```gherkin
  1199→+Given an authenticated user without a Telegram link
  1200→+When POST /api/telegram/link is called
  1201→+Then a new TelegramLinkToken is created with 10-minute expiry
  1202→+And the response contains the token and deep link URL
  1203→+
  1204→+Given an authenticated user with an existing Telegram link
  1205→+When GET /api/telegram/link is called
  1206→+Then the response contains linked: true and chatId
  1207→+
  1208→+Given an authenticated user with an existing Telegram link
  1209→+When DELETE /api/telegram/link is called
  1210→+Then the TelegramLink is deleted
  1211→+And linked: false is returned
  1212→+
  1213→+Given an authenticated user
  1214→+When POST /api/telegram/link is called
  1215→+Then any existing tokens for this user are deleted first (cleanup)
  1216→+```
  1217→+
  1218→+**Implementation**:
  1219→+
  1220→+```typescript
  1221→+import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
  1222→+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
  1223→+import { getAuthenticatedUserId, unauthorizedResponse } from "@/lib/auth-utils"
  1224→+import { randomUUID } from "crypto"
  1225→+
  1226→+const BOT_USERNAME = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_USERNAME || "mypocket_bot"
  1227→+
  1228→+// GET - Check link status
  1229→+export async function GET() {
  1230→+  try {
  1231→+    const userId = await getAuthenticatedUserId()
  1232→+
  1233→+    const link = await prisma.telegramLink.findUnique({
  1234→+      where: { userId },
  1235→+    })
  1236→+
  1237→+    if (link) {
  1238→+      return NextResponse.json({
  1239→+        linked: true,
  1240→+        chatId: link.chatId,
  1241→+        linkedAt: link.linkedAt,
  1242→+      })
  1243→+    }
  1244→+
  1245→+    return NextResponse.json({ linked: false })
  1246→+  } catch (error) {
  1247→+    if (error instanceof Error && error.message === "Unauthorized") {
  1248→+      return unauthorizedResponse()
  1249→+    }
  1250→+    return NextResponse.json(
  1251→+      { error: "Erro ao verificar vinculação" },
  1252→+      { status: 500 }
  1253→+    )
  1254→+  }
  1255→+}
  1256→+
  1257→+// POST - Generate link token
  1258→+export async function POST() {
  1259→+  try {
  1260→+    const userId = await getAuthenticatedUserId()
  1261→+
  1262→+    // Check if already linked
  1263→+    const existingLink = await prisma.telegramLink.findUnique({
  1264→+      where: { userId },
  1265→+    })
  1266→+    if (existingLink) {
  1267→+      return NextResponse.json(
  1268→+        { error: "Conta já vinculada ao Telegram" },
  1269→+        { status: 400 }
  1270→+      )
  1271→+    }
  1272→+
  1273→+    // Delete any existing tokens for this user
  1274→+    await prisma.telegramLinkToken.deleteMany({
  1275→+      where: { userId },
  1276→+    })
  1277→+
  1278→+    // Create new token
  1279→+    const token = randomUUID()
  1280→+    const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000) // 10 minutes
  1281→+
  1282→+    await prisma.telegramLinkToken.create({
  1283→+      data: {
  1284→+        token,
  1285→+        userId,
  1286→+        expiresAt,
  1287→+      },
  1288→+    })
  1289→+
  1290→+    const deepLink = `https://t.me/${BOT_USERNAME}?start=${token}`
  1291→+
  1292→+    return NextResponse.json({
  1293→+      token,
  1294→+      deepLink,
  1295→+      expiresAt,
  1296→+    })
  1297→+  } catch (error) {
  1298→+    if (error instanceof Error && error.message === "Unauthorized") {
  1299→+      return unauthorizedResponse()
  1300→+    }
  1301→+    return NextResponse.json(
  1302→+      { error: "Erro ao gerar link de vinculação" },
  1303→+      { status: 500 }
  1304→+    )
  1305→+  }
  1306→+}
  1307→+
  1308→+// DELETE - Unlink Telegram
  1309→+export async function DELETE() {
  1310→+  try {
  1311→+    const userId = await getAuthenticatedUserId()
  1312→+
  1313→+    await prisma.telegramLink.deleteMany({
  1314→+      where: { userId },
  1315→+    })
  1316→+
  1317→+    return NextResponse.json({ linked: false })
  1318→+  } catch (error) {
  1319→+    if (error instanceof Error && error.message === "Unauthorized") {
  1320→+      return unauthorizedResponse()
  1321→+    }
  1322→+    return NextResponse.json(
  1323→+      { error: "Erro ao desvincular" },
  1324→+      { status: 500 }
  1325→+    )
  1326→+  }
  1327→+}
  1328→+```
  1329→+
  1330→+**Verification**: `npm run build`
  1331→+
  1332→+**On Failure**:
  1333→+- If `randomUUID` not available: Use `crypto.randomUUID()` or `import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid'`
  1334→+- If Prisma model not found: Run `npm run db:generate`
  1335→+
  1336→+**Learning:** O mock do módulo `crypto` nativo no Vitest requer `importOriginal` para preservar o export default. Optei por testar a estrutura da resposta (token presente, deepLink contém o token) em vez de mockar `randomUUID`, abordagem mais robusta e menos frágil.

  1385→+```typescript
  1386→+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
  1387→+import {

  1393→+export async function handleStartCommand(
  1394→+  message: TelegramMessage,
  1395→+  existingUserId: string | null
  1396→+) {
  1397→+  const chatId = message.chat.id
  1398→+  const text = message.text || ""
  1399→+  const token = text.replace("/start", "").trim()
  1400→+
  1401→+  if (!token) {
  1402→+    if (existingUserId) {
  1403→+      return sendMessage(
  1404→+        chatId,
  1405→+        "Sua conta já está vinculada! Envie /menu para ver as opções."
  1406→+      )
  1407→+    }
  1408→+    return sendMessage(
  1409→+      chatId,
  1410→+      "Bem-vindo ao MyPocket Bot!\n\nPara vincular sua conta, acesse Configurações > Telegram no app e clique em \"Vincular Telegram\"."
  1411→+    )
  1412→+  }
  1413→+
  1414→+  // Validate token
  1415→+  const linkToken = await prisma.telegramLinkToken.findUnique({
  1416→+    where: { token },
  1417→+  })
  1418→+
  1419→+  if (!linkToken) {
  1420→+    return sendMessage(chatId, "Token inválido. Gere um novo link nas configurações do app.")
  1421→+  }
  1422→+
  1423→+  if (linkToken.expiresAt < new Date()) {
  1424→+    await prisma.telegramLinkToken.delete({ where: { id: linkToken.id } })
  1425→+    return sendMessage(chatId, "Token expirado. Gere um novo link nas configurações do app.")
  1426→+  }
  1427→+
  1428→+  // Check if chatId already linked to another account
  1429→+  const existingChatLink = await prisma.telegramLink.findUnique({
  1430→+    where: { chatId: String(chatId) },
  1431→+  })
  1432→+  if (existingChatLink) {
  1433→+    return sendMessage(
  1434→+      chatId,
  1435→+      "Este chat já está vinculado a outra conta. Desvincule primeiro com /desvincular."
  1436→+    )
  1437→+  }
  1438→+
  1439→+  // Create link and delete token
  1440→+  try {
  1441→+    await prisma.$transaction([
  1442→+      prisma.telegramLink.create({
  1443→+        data: {
  1444→+          userId: linkToken.userId,
  1445→+          chatId: String(chatId),
  1446→+        },
  1447→+      }),
  1448→+      prisma.telegramLinkToken.delete({ where: { id: linkToken.id } }),
  1449→+    ])
  1450→+
  1451→+    return sendMessage(
  1452→+      chatId,
  1453→+      "Conta vinculada com sucesso! 🎉\n\nEnvie /menu para ver as opções disponíveis."
  1454→+    )
  1455→+  } catch (error) {
  1456→+    // Handle unique constraint violation (user already linked)
  1457→+    return sendMessage(
  1458→+      chatId,
  1459→+      "Erro ao vincular conta. Sua conta pode já estar vinculada a outro chat."
  1460→+    )
  1461→+  }
  1462→+}

Date 1794→+} 1795→+ 1796→+export function parseExpenseMessage(text: string): ParsedExpense | null { 1797→+ const trimmed = text.trim() 1798→+ if (!trimmed) return null 1799→+ 1800→+ // We'll track parts to remove by position 1801→+ const removals: Array<{ start: number; end: number }> = [] 1802→+ 1803→+ // Extract date (DD/MM/YYYY) — look for last occurrence 1804→+ const dateRegex = /(\d{2})\/(\d{2})\/(\d{4})/g 1805→+ let dateMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null 1806→+ let lastDateMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null 1807→+ while ((dateMatch = dateRegex.exec(trimmed)) !== null) { 1808→+ lastDateMatch = dateMatch 1809→+ } 1810→+ 1811→+ let date: Date 1812→+ if (lastDateMatch) { 1813→+ const [, day, month, year] = lastDateMatch 1814→+ date = new Date(parseInt(year), parseInt(month) - 1, parseInt(day), 12, 0, 0) 1815→+ removals.push({ start: lastDateMatch.index, end: lastDateMatch.index + lastDateMatch[0].length }) 1816→+ } else { 1817→+ const now = new Date() 1818→+ date = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth(), now.getDate(), 12, 0, 0) 1819→+ } 1820→+ 1821→+ // Extract amount — look for Brazilian number format (last occurrence) 1822→+ // Matches: 45,90 | 1.234,56 | 1234,56 1823→+ const amountRegex = /(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{3})*,\d{2}|\d+,\d{2})/g 1824→+ let amountMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null 1825→+ let lastAmountMatch: RegExpExecArray | null = null 1826→+ while ((amountMatch = amountRegex.exec(trimmed)) !== null) { 1827→+ lastAmountMatch = amountMatch 1828→+ } 1829→+ 1830→+ if (!lastAmountMatch) return null 1831→+ 1832→+ const amountStr = lastAmountMatch[1] 1833→+ const amount = parseFloat(amountStr.replace(/\./g, "").replace(",", ".")) 1834→+ if (isNaN(amount) || amount <= 0) return null 1835→+ 1836→+ removals.push({ start: lastAmountMatch.index, end: lastAmountMatch.index + lastAmountMatch[0].length }) 1837→+ 1838→+ // Build description by removing matched parts by position (sorted descending to preserve indices) 1839→+ let description = trimmed 1840→+ removals.sort((a, b) => b.start - a.start) 1841→+ for (const { start, end } of removals) { 1842→+ description = description.slice(0, start) + description.slice(end) 1843→+ } 1844→+ description = description.replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim() 1845→+ 1846→+ if (!description) return null 1847→+ 1848→+ return { description, amount, date } 1849→+} 1850→+``` 1851→+   2356→+# Phase 5: CSV Import + Consultas
  2357→+
  2358→+## Overview
  2359→+
  2360→+Implementar importação de arquivos CSV via bot (download, parse, dedup, criação) e os três comandos de consulta (resumo do mês, gastos por categoria, últimas transações) com menu via inline keyboard. Esta fase completa todas as funcionalidades do bot.

  2372→+## Changes Required
  2373→+
  2374→+#### 1. Implement CSV file import via bot -- DONE
  2375→+
  2376→+**File**: `src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` (MODIFY)
  2377→+**Complexity**: High
  2378→+**TDD**: NO — integrates Telegram File API + existing parseCSV + database writes
  2379→+**Depends On**: Phase 4 (commands.ts with callback query handler), Phase 1 (dedup module)
  2380→+- **Learning:** Consolidei os imports de `./client` em um único bloco para evitar imports duplicados. O `parseCSV` retorna `ImportedTransaction[]` com `amount` já negativo (credit card expenses), então o dedup precisa considerar isso ao verificar duplicatas.
  2381→+
  2382→+**Load Before Implementing**:
  2383→+1. `src/lib/telegram/commands.ts` (full file) - Current state after Phase 4
  2384→+2. `src/lib/telegram/client.ts` (full file) - getFile, downloadFile functions
  2385→+3. `src/lib/csv-parser.ts` (lines 151-242) - parseCSV function signature and return type
  2386→+4. `src/lib/dedup.ts` (full file) - findDuplicate function

  2388→+
  2389→+**Pre-conditions**:
  2390→+- [x] Phase 4 completed (commands.ts has working callback query handler)
  2391→+- [x] `src/lib/csv-parser.ts` exports `parseCSV(fileContent: string, origin: string)`

  2393→+
  2394→+**Why**: Permite importar faturas CSV diretamente pelo Telegram. O bot baixa o arquivo, parseia, verifica duplicatas, e cria transações — replicando o fluxo do app web.
  2395→+
  2396→+**Acceptance Criteria**:
  2397→+```gherkin
  2398→+Given a linked user sends a .csv file via Telegram
  2399→+When handleDocumentMessage is called
  2400→+Then the file is downloaded and parsed
  2401→+And a summary message shows count of transactions found and duplicates detected
  2402→+And inline keyboard offers [Importar] [Cancelar]
  2403→+
  2404→+Given the user clicks [Importar] after sending a CSV
  2405→+When handleCallbackQuery receives csv_confirm
  2406→+Then all non-duplicate transactions are created in the database
  2407→+And a success message shows imported count and skipped duplicates
  2408→+
  2409→+Given the user sends a non-CSV file
  2410→+When handleDocumentMessage is called
  2411→+Then an error message says only CSV files are supported
  2412→+```
  2413→+
  2414→+**Implementation**:
  2415→+
  2416→+Add import at top of commands.ts:
  2417→+```typescript
  2418→+import { parseCSV } from "@/lib/csv-parser"
  2419→+import { getFile, downloadFile } from "./client"
  2420→+```
  2421→+
  2422→+Replace `handleDocumentMessage`:
  2423→+```typescript
  2424→+// Pending imports are stored in database (TelegramPendingImport model)
  2425→+// to survive cold starts and work across multiple processes
  2426→+
  2427→+export async function handleDocumentMessage(
  2428→+  message: TelegramMessage,
  2429→+  userId: string
  2430→+) {
  2431→+  const chatId = message.chat.id
  2432→+  const doc = message.document
  2433→+
  2434→+  if (!doc) return
  2435→+
  2436→+  // Check file extension
  2437→+  const fileName = doc.file_name || ""
  2438→+  if (!fileName.toLowerCase().endsWith(".csv")) {
  2439→+    return sendMessage(chatId, "Apenas arquivos CSV são suportados.")
  2440→+  }
  2441→+
  2442→+  // Check file size (max 5MB to be safe)
  2443→+  if (doc.file_size && doc.file_size > 5 * 1024 * 1024) {
  2444→+    return sendMessage(chatId, "Arquivo muito grande. Máximo 5MB.")
  2445→+  }
  2446→+
  2447→+  try {
  2448→+    // Download file
  2449→+    const filePath = await getFile(doc.file_id)
  2450→+    if (!filePath) {
  2451→+      return sendMessage(chatId, "Erro ao acessar o arquivo.")
  2452→+    }
  2453→+
  2454→+    const content = await downloadFile(filePath)
  2455→+
  2456→+    // Parse CSV
  2457→+    const origin = "Telegram"
  2458→+    const parsed = await parseCSV(content, origin)
  2459→+
  2460→+    if (parsed.length === 0) {
  2461→+      return sendMessage(chatId, "Nenhuma transação encontrada no arquivo.")
  2462→+    }
  2463→+
  2464→+    // Check for duplicates
  2465→+    let duplicateCount = 0
  2466→+    const uniqueTransactions: typeof parsed = []
  2467→+
  2468→+    for (const t of parsed) {
  2469→+      const amount = t.type === "EXPENSE" ? -Math.abs(t.amount) : Math.abs(t.amount)
  2470→+      const dup = await findDuplicate({
  2471→+        userId,
  2472→+        description: t.description,
  2473→+        amount,
  2474→+        date: t.date,
  2475→+      })
  2476→+      if (dup) {
  2477→+        duplicateCount++
  2478→+      } else {
  2479→+        uniqueTransactions.push(t)
  2480→+      }
  2481→+    }
  2482→+
  2483→+    if (uniqueTransactions.length === 0) {
  2484→+      return sendMessage(
  2485→+        chatId,
  2486→+        `Todas as ${parsed.length} transações já existem no sistema.`
  2487→+      )
  2488→+    }
  2489→+
  2490→+    // Store for confirmation in database
  2491→+    // Clean expired entries first
  2492→+    await prisma.telegramPendingImport.deleteMany({
  2493→+      where: { expiresAt: { lt: new Date() } },
  2494→+    })
  2495→+
  2496→+    const pendingImport = await prisma.telegramPendingImport.create({
  2497→+      data: {
  2498→+        userId,
  2499→+        chatId: String(chatId),

2500→+        payload: JSON.stringify(uniqueTransactions.map(t => ({
  2501→+          description: t.description,
  2502→+          amount: t.amount,
  2503→+          date: t.date,
  2504→+          categoryId: t.suggestedCategoryId || null,
  2505→+          isInstallment: t.isInstallment || false,
  2506→+          currentInstallment: t.currentInstallment || null,
  2507→+          totalInstallments: t.totalInstallments || null,
  2508→+          type: t.type || "EXPENSE",
  2509→+        }))),
  2510→+        origin,
  2511→+        expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 10 * 60 * 1000),
  2512→+      },
  2513→+    })
  2514→+
  2515→+    const summaryLines = [
  2516→+      `📄 Arquivo: ${fileName}`,
  2517→+      `📊 ${parsed.length} transações encontradas`,
  2518→+    ]
  2519→+    if (duplicateCount > 0) {
  2520→+      summaryLines.push(`⚠️ ${duplicateCount} duplicatas ignoradas`)
  2521→+    }
  2522→+    summaryLines.push(`✅ ${uniqueTransactions.length} prontas para importar`)
  2523→+
  2524→+    return sendMessage(chatId, summaryLines.join("\n"), {
  2525→+      reply_markup: {
  2526→+        inline_keyboard: [
  2527→+          [
  2528→+            { text: "✅ Importar", callback_data: `csv_confirm:${pendingImport.id}` },
  2529→+            { text: "❌ Cancelar", callback_data: `csv_cancel:0` },
  2530→+          ],
  2531→+        ],
  2532→+      },
  2533→+    })
  2534→+  } catch (error) {
  2535→+    console.error("CSV import error:", error)
  2536→+    return sendMessage(
  2537→+      chatId,
  2538→+      "Erro ao processar o arquivo. Verifique se é um CSV válido (C6, Itaú ou BTG)."
  2539→+    )
  2540→+  }
  2541→+}
  2542→+
  2543→+export async function handleCsvConfirm(
  2544→+  chatId: number,
  2545→+  messageId: number,
  2546→+  userId: string,
  2547→+  importId: string
  2548→+) {
  2549→+  const pending = await prisma.telegramPendingImport.findUnique({
  2550→+    where: { id: importId },
  2551→+  })
  2552→+  if (!pending || pending.expiresAt < new Date()) {
  2553→+    if (pending) await prisma.telegramPendingImport.delete({ where: { id: importId } })
  2554→+    return editMessageText(chatId, messageId, "Importação expirada. Envie o arquivo novamente.")
  2555→+  }
  2556→+
  2557→+  await prisma.telegramPendingImport.delete({ where: { id: importId } })
  2558→+
  2559→+  const transactions = JSON.parse(pending.payload) as Array<{
  2560→+    description: string
  2561→+    amount: number
  2562→+    date: string | Date
  2563→+    categoryId: string | null
  2564→+    isInstallment: boolean
  2565→+    currentInstallment: number | null
  2566→+    totalInstallments: number | null
  2567→+    type: string
  2568→+  }>
  2569→+
  2570→+  let importedCount = 0
  2571→+  for (const t of transactions) {
  2572→+    const type = t.type || "EXPENSE"
  2573→+    let amount = t.amount
  2574→+    if (type === "EXPENSE" && amount > 0) amount = -amount
  2575→+    else if (type === "INCOME" && amount < 0) amount = Math.abs(amount)
  2576→+
  2577→+    const dateStr = typeof t.date === "string" && !(t.date as string).includes("T")
  2578→+      ? t.date + "T12:00:00"
  2579→+      : t.date
  2580→+    const transactionDate = new Date(dateStr)
  2581→+
  2582→+    await prisma.transaction.create({
  2583→+      data: {
  2584→+        userId,
  2585→+        description: t.description,
  2586→+        amount,
  2587→+        date: transactionDate,
  2588→+        type,
  2589→+        origin: pending!.origin,
  2590→+        categoryId: t.categoryId,
  2591→+        isFixed: false,
  2592→+        isInstallment: t.isInstallment,
  2593→+        currentInstallment: t.currentInstallment,
  2594→+        totalInstallments: t.totalInstallments,
  2595→+      },
  2596→+    })
  2597→+    importedCount++
  2598→+  }
  2599→+
  2600→+  return editMessageText(
  2601→+    chatId,
  2602→+    messageId,
  2603→+    `✅ ${importedCount} transações importadas com sucesso!`
  2604→+  )
  2605→+}
  2606→+```
  2607→+
  2608→+Update `handleCallbackQuery` to route csv_confirm:
  2609→+```typescript
  2610→+  if (data.startsWith("csv_confirm:")) {
  2611→+    const importKey = data.replace("csv_confirm:", "")
  2612→+    return handleCsvConfirm(chatId, messageId, userId, importKey)
  2613→+  }
  2614→+```
  2615→+

  2619→+- If `parseCSV` signature mismatch: Check `csv-parser.ts` for exact parameter names
  2620→+- If callback_data exceeds 64 bytes: Shorten importKey format
  2621→+- If pendingCsvImports memory grows: The cleanup loop handles expiration

  2643→+**Why**: Três consultas core do bot: resumo mensal (totais + orçamento geral), gastos por categoria (vs orçamento), e últimas 10 transações.
  2644→+
  2645→+**Acceptance Criteria**:
  2646→+```gherkin
  2647→+Given a linked user requests summary
  2648→+When handleSummaryQuery is called
  2649→+Then it returns formatted text with income, expense, balance, and budget usage
  2650→+
  2651→+Given a linked user requests category breakdown
  2652→+When handleCategoryQuery is called
  2653→+Then it returns each category with spent amount vs budget (if defined)
  2654→+
  2655→+Given a linked user requests recent transactions
  2656→+When handleTransactionsQuery is called
  2657→+Then it returns the 10 most recent transactions with date, description, amount, and category
  2658→+```
  2659→+
  2660→+**Implementation**:
  2661→+
  2662→+`src/lib/telegram/queries.ts`:
  2663→+```typescript
  2664→+import prisma from "@/lib/db"
  2665→+import { formatCurrency } from "@/lib/utils"
  2666→+import { sendMessage, type InlineKeyboardButton } from "./client"
  2667→+
  2668→+export async function handleSummaryQuery(chatId: number, userId: string) {
  2669→+  const now = new Date()
  2670→+  const month = now.getMonth() + 1
  2671→+  const year = now.getFullYear()
  2672→+
  2673→+  const monthStart = new Date(year, month - 1, 1)
  2674→+  const monthEnd = new Date(year, month, 0, 23, 59, 59, 999)
  2675→+
  2676→+  // Fetch transactions for current month
  2677→+  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
  2678→+    where: {
  2679→+      userId,
  2680→+      date: { gte: monthStart, lte: monthEnd },
  2681→+      deletedAt: null,
  2682→+    },
  2683→+    include: { investmentTransaction: true },
  2684→+  })
  2685→+
  2686→+  // Calculate totals (same logic as /api/summary)
  2687→+  let income = 0
  2688→+  let expense = 0
  2689→+  for (const t of transactions) {
  2690→+    if ((t as Record<string, unknown>).investmentTransaction) continue
  2691→+    if (t.type === "TRANSFER") continue
  2692→+    if (t.type === "INCOME") income += Math.abs(t.amount)
  2693→+    else if (t.type === "EXPENSE") expense += Math.abs(t.amount)
  2694→+  }
  2695→+  const balance = income - expense
  2696→+
  2697→+  // Get total budget
  2698→+  const budgets = await prisma.budget.findMany({
  2699→+    where: { userId, isActive: true },
  2700→+  })
  2701→+  const totalBudget = budgets.reduce((sum, b) => sum + b.amount, 0)
  2702→+
  2703→+  const monthName = monthStart.toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", { month: "long", year: "numeric" })
  2704→+
  2705→+  const lines = [
  2706→+    `📊 Resumo - ${monthName}`,
  2707→+    "",
  2708→+    `Despesas:  ${formatCurrency(expense)}`,
  2709→+    `Receitas:  ${formatCurrency(income)}`,
  2710→+    `Saldo:     ${formatCurrency(balance)}`,
  2711→+  ]
  2712→+
  2713→+  if (totalBudget > 0) {
  2714→+    const pct = Math.round((expense / totalBudget) * 100)
  2715→+    const filled = Math.round(pct / 10)
  2716→+    const bar = "█".repeat(Math.min(filled, 10)) + "░".repeat(Math.max(10 - filled, 0))
  2717→+    lines.push("")
  2718→+    lines.push(`Orçamento: ${formatCurrency(totalBudget)}`)
  2719→+    lines.push(`Usado:     ${pct}% ${bar}`)
  2720→+  }
  2721→+
  2722→+  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
  2723→+    reply_markup: {
  2724→+      inline_keyboard: [[{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }]],
  2725→+    },
  2726→+  })
  2727→+}
  2728→+
  2729→+export async function handleCategoryQuery(chatId: number, userId: string) {
  2730→+  const now = new Date()
  2731→+  const month = now.getMonth() + 1
  2732→+  const year = now.getFullYear()
  2733→+
  2734→+  const monthStart = new Date(year, month - 1, 1)
  2735→+  const monthEnd = new Date(year, month, 0, 23, 59, 59, 999)
  2736→+
  2737→+  // Fetch expense transactions with categories
  2738→+  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
  2739→+    where: {
  2740→+      userId,
  2741→+      type: "EXPENSE",
  2742→+      date: { gte: monthStart, lte: monthEnd },
  2743→+      deletedAt: null,
  2744→+    },
  2745→+    include: { category: true },
  2746→+  })
  2747→+
  2748→+  // Aggregate by category
  2749→+  const categoryTotals = new Map<string, { name: string; total: number }>()
  2750→+  for (const t of transactions) {
  2751→+    const catName = t.category?.name || "Sem categoria"
  2752→+    const catId = t.categoryId || "none"
  2753→+    const existing = categoryTotals.get(catId) || { name: catName, total: 0 }
  2754→+    existing.total += Math.abs(t.amount)
  2755→+    categoryTotals.set(catId, existing)
  2756→+  }
  2757→+
  2758→+  // Fetch budgets
  2759→+  const budgets = await prisma.budget.findMany({
  2760→+    where: { userId, isActive: true },
  2761→+    include: { category: true },
  2762→+  })
  2763→+  const budgetMap = new Map(budgets.map(b => [b.categoryId, b.amount]))
  2764→+
  2765→+  const monthName = monthStart.toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", { month: "long", year: "numeric" })
  2766→+
  2767→+  const lines = [`🏷️ Gastos por categoria - ${monthName}`, ""]
  2768→+
  2769→+  // Sort by total descending
  2770→+  const sorted = [...categoryTotals.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1].total - a[1].total)
  2771→+
  2772→+  for (const [catId, { name, total }] of sorted) {
  2773→+    const budget = budgetMap.get(catId)
  2774→+    if (budget) {
  2775→+      const pct = Math.round((total / budget) * 100)
  2776→+      const warning = pct >= 100 ? " ⚠️" : ""
  2777→+      lines.push(`${name}: ${formatCurrency(total)} / ${formatCurrency(budget)} (${pct}%)${warning}`)
  2778→+    } else {
  2779→+      lines.push(`${name}: ${formatCurrency(total)}`)
  2780→+    }
  2781→+  }
  2782→+
  2783→+  if (sorted.length === 0) {
  2784→+    lines.push("Nenhuma despesa neste mês.")
  2785→+  }
  2786→+
  2787→+  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
  2788→+    reply_markup: {
  2789→+      inline_keyboard: [[{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }]],
  2790→+    },
  2791→+  })
  2792→+}
  2793→+
  2794→+export async function handleTransactionsQuery(
  2795→+  chatId: number,
  2796→+  userId: string,
  2797→+  offset: number = 0
  2798→+) {
  2799→+  const limit = 10
  2800→+  const transactions = await prisma.transaction.findMany({
  2801→+    where: {
  2802→+      userId,
  2803→+      deletedAt: null,
  2804→+    },
  2805→+    include: { category: true },
  2806→+    orderBy: { date: "desc" },
  2807→+    skip: offset,
  2808→+    take: limit + 1, // Fetch one extra to check if there are more
  2809→+  })
  2810→+
  2811→+  const hasMore = transactions.length > limit
  2812→+  const display = transactions.slice(0, limit)
  2813→+
  2814→+  const lines = [`📋 ${offset === 0 ? "Últimas" : "Mais"} transações`, ""]
  2815→+
  2816→+  for (const t of display) {
  2817→+    const date = new Date(t.date).toLocaleDateString("pt-BR", {
  2818→+      day: "2-digit",
  2819→+      month: "2-digit",
  2820→+    })
  2821→+    const sign = t.type === "INCOME" ? "+" : "-"
  2822→+    const amount = formatCurrency(Math.abs(t.amount))
  2823→+    const cat = t.category?.name || ""
  2824→+    lines.push(`${date} ${t.description.substring(0, 25)}  ${sign}${amount}  ${cat}`)
  2825→+  }
  2826→+
  2827→+  if (display.length === 0) {
  2828→+    lines.push("Nenhuma transação encontrada.")
  2829→+  }
  2830→+
  2831→+  const keyboard: InlineKeyboardButton[][] = []
  2832→+  const navRow: InlineKeyboardButton[] = []
  2833→+  if (offset > 0) {
  2834→+    navRow.push({ text: "⬅️ Anteriores", callback_data: `txns:${Math.max(0, offset - limit)}` })
  2835→+  }
  2836→+  if (hasMore) {
  2837→+    navRow.push({ text: "➡️ Próximas", callback_data: `txns:${offset + limit}` })
  2838→+  }
  2839→+  if (navRow.length > 0) keyboard.push(navRow)
  2840→+  keyboard.push([{ text: "🔙 Menu", callback_data: "menu:0" }])
  2841→+
  2842→+  return sendMessage(chatId, lines.join("\n"), {
  2843→+    reply_markup: { inline_keyboard: keyboard },
  2844→+  })
  2845→+}
  2846→+```
  2847→+

  2851→+- If `investmentTransaction` relation not found: Check Transaction model includes this relation; may need to filter differently
  2852→+- If formatCurrency import fails: Verify `@/lib/utils` path and export name
